content_blocks.scene_description: Mike and Wu Binbin are at a shoe store, trying on shoes and comparing their sizes and weights.

key_points.english: Your feet are bigger than mine.

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Those shoes in the window are nice! Let's have a look.

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: What size are your shoes, Mike?

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Size 7.

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Size 7? That's so small.

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: In China I wear size 40 shoes.

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Oh! Your feet are bigger than mine. My shoes are size 37.
dialogues.chinese: 哦！你的脚比我的大。我的鞋是37码的。

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Really? You look taller than me.
dialogues.chinese: 真的吗？你看起来比我高。

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: But I think you are heavier. How heavy are you?
dialogues.chinese: 但我认为你比我重。你有多重？

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: I'm 48 kilograms.

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Yes, you're heavier. I'm 46 kilograms.

content_blocks.scene_description: Wu Binbin and his family are going on a trip to the countryside. Wu Binbin has a story to tell Robin.

key_points.english: It is getting lower and lower, but his shadow is getting longer and longer.
key_points.chinese: 它变得越来越低，但是他的影子变得越来越长。

key_points.english: You are older and smarter than me.

paragraphs.english: Little Duck is watching the sun go down. It is getting lower and lower, but his shadow is getting longer and longer.
paragraphs.chinese: 小鸭子正在看日落。太阳变得越来越低，但是他的影子变得越来越长。

paragraphs.english: "Why is that?" Little Duck asks his friend Old Tree. "You are older and smarter than me. Tell me — why is my shadow longer when the sun gets lower?"
paragraphs.chinese: “那是为什么呢？”小鸭子问他的朋友老树。“你比我更老、更聪明。告诉我——为什么当太阳变得更低时，我的影子更长？”

paragraphs.english: Old Tree says, "That's easy, Little Duck. The sun goes down every day, and we grow older every day. Your shadow grows longer because you are growing taller. You are becoming a big beautiful bird!"
paragraphs.chinese: 老树说：“那很简单，小鸭子。太阳每天落下，我们每天都在变老。你的影子变长是因为你长高了。你正在变成一只美丽的大鸟！”

content_blocks.scene_description: Wu Binbin asks Robin a question about the story.

dialogues.english: Can you answer Little Duck's question, Robin?
dialogues.chinese: 你能回答小鸭子的问题吗，罗宾？

content_blocks.scene_description: The animals are holding an audition to find a new goalkeeper for their football team.

dialogues.speaker: Sign
dialogues.english: We want a goalkeeper !!

dialogues.speaker: Zebra
dialogues.english: They need a new goalkeeper.
dialogues.chinese: 他们需要一个新的守门员。

dialogues.speaker: Deer
dialogues.english: I'd like to have a try.

dialogues.speaker: Giraffe
dialogues.english: Me too.

dialogues.speaker: Dog
dialogues.english: I can't wait.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: How tall are you?

dialogues.speaker: Deer
dialogues.english: I'm 1.8 metres.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: How heavy are you?

dialogues.speaker: Deer
dialogues.english: I'm 86 kilograms.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Mr Zebra is 10 centimetres taller than Mr Deer.
dialogues.chinese: 斑马先生比鹿先生高10厘米。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Yes, and he's stronger. But he can't catch the ball!
dialogues.chinese: 是的，而且他更强壮。但是他接不住球！

dialogues.speaker: Giraffe
dialogues.english: I'm the tallest one.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Sorry. Your neck is too long. The others can't reach you. Football needs teamwork.
dialogues.chinese: 对不起。你的脖子太长了。其他人够不到你。足球需要团队合作。

dialogues.speaker: Hippo
dialogues.english: What's happening here?

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Oh! Here's the one we want!
dialogues.chinese: 哦！这就是我们想要的人选！

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Why? He's too heavy!

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: We need a big guy!

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Oh! He's almost bigger than the goal!
dialogues.chinese: 哦！他简直比球门还要大！

dialogues.speaker: Dog
dialogues.english: Yes! He's an excellent goalkeeper.
dialogues.chinese: 是的！他是个出色的守门员。

dialogues.english: That's nice. Did you do anything else?

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Yes, I cleaned my room and washed my clothes.

content_blocks.scene_description: John and Amy are talking at a bus stop.

key_points.english: What did you do last weekend?
I stayed at home all weekend and slept.
key_points.chinese: 我整个周末都待在家里睡觉。

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: Hey, Amy. Let's go to the bookstore. I want to buy the new film magazine.
dialogues.chinese: 嘿，艾米。我们去书店吧。我想买那本新的电影杂志。

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Oh, I read it last weekend.

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: Was it interesting?

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Yes, it talked about a lot of new films. What did you do last weekend? Did you see a film?
dialogues.chinese: 是的，它谈论了很多新电影。你上个周末做了什么？你看电影了吗？

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: No, I had a cold. I stayed at home all weekend and slept.
dialogues.chinese: 没有，我感冒了。我整个周末都待在家里睡觉。

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Oh, I'm sorry. I'm happy you feel better now.
dialogues.chinese: 哦，很抱歉。我很高兴你现在感觉好些了。

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: Thanks. Let's go by bus. It's faster than walking.
dialogues.chinese: 谢谢。我们乘公共汽车去吧。那比走路快。

content_blocks.scene_description: An introduction to Mrs Broom's job and a feedback letter from Wu Binbin's family about their bad stay at the Holiday Hotel.

key_points.english: What do you think she did yesterday?
key_points.chinese: 你认为她昨天做了什么？

paragraphs.english: This is Mrs Broom. She works in a hotel. She is a housekeeper. What do you think she did yesterday?
paragraphs.chinese: 这是布鲁姆夫人。她在一家酒店工作。她是一名客房清洁员。你认为她昨天做了什么？

paragraphs.english:
made the beds
cooked the food
cleaned the rooms
paragraphs.chinese:
整理床铺
做饭
打扫房间

paragraphs.english: Wu Binbin and his family stayed at the Holiday Hotel. It was not a happy time.
paragraphs.chinese: 吴彬彬和他的家人住在假日酒店。那不是一段愉快的时光。

paragraphs.english:
Dear Customer,
Thank you for staying in our hotel. How was your stay?
Please circle:
Good Average Bad (Bad is circled)
paragraphs.chinese:
亲爱的顾客，
感谢您入住我们的酒店。您住得怎么样？
请圈出：
好 一般 差（“差”被圈出）

paragraphs.english: Comments
paragraphs.chinese: 意见

paragraphs.english:
Dear Sir,
Our weekend at your hotel was bad. Our room was big but everything was very old. Our friend Robin cleaned our room and fixed a broken chair.
paragraphs.chinese:
亲爱的先生，
我们在你们酒店的周末很糟糕。我们的房间很大，但所有的东西都很旧。我们的朋友罗宾打扫了我们的房间，并修理了一把破椅子。

paragraphs.english: My mother wanted to read a book but the lamp was too small. My dad got some hamburgers from the hotel kitchen, but they were cold and tasted bad.
paragraphs.chinese: 我妈妈想看书，但是台灯太小了。我爸爸从酒店厨房拿了一些汉堡包，但是它们是冷的，而且味道很差。

paragraphs.english: I wanted to watch TV but the TV didn't work. The people in Room 301 listened to loud music. I didn't sleep all night.
paragraphs.chinese: 我想看电视，但是电视坏了。301房间的人听着很大声的音乐。我整晚都没睡。

paragraphs.english:
I'm sorry, but we didn't enjoy our stay very much.
Wu Binbin and family
paragraphs.chinese:
很抱歉，但我们对这次入住不是很满意。
吴彬彬和家人

content_blocks.scene_description: Wu Binbin's diary about his day with his family and their new dog Max.

paragraphs.english: Today was a sunny day. In the morning, we rode a bike for three people. Max sat in a basket on the front of the bike. That was fun! We took pictures of the beautiful countryside. We bought some gifts and ate some delicious food.
paragraphs.chinese: 今天是个晴天。在早上，我们骑了一辆三人自行车。马克斯坐在自行车前面的篮子里。那很有趣！我们拍了美丽乡村的照片。我们买了一些礼物，吃了一些美味的食物。

paragraphs.english: In the afternoon, Mum ate some bad fruit and didn't feel well. So we stayed in the hotel. Dad and I wanted to make her happy. We dressed up and made a funny play. Robin played the part of a dog. He was so cute. Max liked him so much. He jumped on him and licked him. Of course, Robin didn't like it. We laughed and laughed.
paragraphs.chinese: 在下午，妈妈吃了一些坏水果，感觉不舒服。所以我们待在酒店里。爸爸和我想让她开心。我们打扮起来，演了一出滑稽的戏。罗宾扮演了一只狗。他太可爱了。马克斯非常喜欢他。他跳到他身上舔他。当然，罗宾不喜欢这样。我们笑啊笑。

paragraphs.english: It was a bad day but also a good day!
paragraphs.chinese: 这是糟糕的一天，但也是美好的一天！

dialogues.speaker: Grandpa
dialogues.english: Wow, a library! There was no library in my old school.

dialogues.english: Tell us about your school, please.

dialogues.english: Me too. One day I'm going to visit the moon.
dialogues.chinese: 我也是。有一天我要去参观月球。

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: The Americans took about five days to get there in 1969.
dialogues.chinese: 美国人在1969年花了大约五天时间到达那里。

dialogues.english: How do you know that?

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Easy, I looked it up on the Internet.
dialogues.chinese: 很简单，我在互联网上查的。

dialogues.speaker: Grandpa
dialogues.english: Ah! There were no computers or Internet in my time.

content_blocks.scene_description: Chen Jie, John and Mike are looking at Mike's old photos and talking about how they have changed.

key_points.english: Before, I was quiet. Now, I'm very active in class.
key_points.chinese: 以前，我很安静。现在，我在课堂上很活跃。

key_points.english: I was short, so I couldn't ride my bike well.

dialogues.speaker: Chen Jie
dialogues.english: John, come and look at Mike's old photos!
dialogues.chinese: 约翰，快来看迈克的旧照片！

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: That's funny, Mike! You're wearing a pink T-shirt.
dialogues.chinese: 真有趣，迈克！你穿着一件粉色的T恤。

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Yes, but I don't like pink now.
dialogues.chinese: 是的，但我现在不喜欢粉色了。

dialogues.speaker: Chen Jie
dialogues.english: We are all different now!

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Right. Before, I was quiet. Now, I'm very active in class. How about you?
dialogues.chinese: 对。以前，我很安静。现在，我在课堂上很活跃。你呢？

dialogues.speaker: John
dialogues.english: Well, I was short, so I couldn't ride my bike well. Now, I go cycling every day. I like it very much.
dialogues.chinese: 嗯，我以前很矮，所以我自行车骑得不好。现在，我每天都去骑自行车。我非常喜欢它。

questions.q: 1. What animal can run very fast?

content_blocks.scene_description: A story about Wu Binbin's dream where he races with his father, Max, and a cheetah.

key_points.english: Understand a narrative story in the past tense.
key_points.chinese: 理解一篇过去时的叙事故事。

paragraphs.english: Wu Binbin had a race with his father and Max. There were many people there. His father ran very fast, but Wu Binbin could not. There was nothing he could do. He was so worried. "I could run fast at school," he thought. "Why am I so slow now?"
paragraphs.chinese: 吴彬彬和他的爸爸以及马克斯进行了一场赛跑。那里有很多人。他的爸爸跑得非常快，但是吴彬彬却不能。他无能为力。他非常担心。“我在学校能跑得很快，”他想。“为什么我现在这么慢？”

paragraphs.english: Then Robin said, "Drink this!" and gave Wu Binbin some water. Wu Binbin drank it and suddenly he felt good. He could run fast again.
paragraphs.chinese: 然后罗宾说：“喝这个！”并给了吴彬彬一些水。吴彬彬喝了它，突然他感觉很好。他又可以跑得很快了。

paragraphs.english: There was a second race. There were many animals in that race. Wu Binbin ran like a cheetah. He could win the race! But suddenly he tripped and fell. Then he woke up. He was in bed. It was all a dream!
paragraphs.chinese: 进行了第二场比赛。那场比赛中有很多动物。吴彬彬跑得像猎豹一样。他能赢得比赛！但是突然他绊倒了。然后他醒了。他在床上。这都是一场梦！

content_blocks.instructions: Number the pictures.

content_blocks.scene_description: Zip和Zoom通过观看三组关于过去、现在和未来的对比画面（人类烹饪方式的演变、地球环境的变化、鲸鱼生存状态的改变），讨论人类生活、环境保护及动物保护的主题。

key_points.english: Understand the changes in human life and the environment over time.
key_points.chinese: 理解人类生活和环境随时间的演变。

key_points.english: We should help the earth now to make it a better place.
key_points.chinese: 我们现在应该保护地球，让它成为一个更好的地方。

paragraphs.english: Cavemen often ate raw meat. They didn't cook their food.
paragraphs.chinese: 穴居人经常吃生肉。他们不烹饪食物。

paragraphs.english: Now there are many things to help you cook.
paragraphs.chinese: 现在有许多东西可以帮助你烹饪。

paragraphs.english: In 2050, there will be house robots. They will cook, clean and wash the dishes.
paragraphs.chinese: 在2050年，将会有家用机器人。它们将会做饭、打扫卫生和洗碗。

paragraphs.english: Zip: Oh, cavemen didn't cook their meat?
paragraphs.chinese: Zip：哦，穴居人不烹饪他们的肉吗？

paragraphs.english: Zoom: It's OK. They didn't do the dishes either!
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom：没关系。他们也不洗碗！

paragraphs.english: There were many trees, and there were many clean rivers.
paragraphs.chinese: 过去有许多树，也有许多干净的河流。

paragraphs.english: Now, there are more and more buildings.
paragraphs.chinese: 现在，建筑越来越多了。

paragraphs.english: We should help the earth now, so birds will have homes and the forests will be healthy and green again.
paragraphs.chinese: 我们现在应该帮助地球，这样鸟儿就会有家，森林也会再次变得健康和翠绿。

paragraphs.english: Zip: Look! How beautiful the world was! Oh! What will the birds do?
paragraphs.chinese: Zip：看！世界过去多美啊！哦！鸟儿们该怎么办？

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Don't worry. People will help them.
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom：别担心。人们会帮助它们的。

paragraphs.english: Before humans lived on the earth, whales already lived in the sea.
paragraphs.chinese: 在人类在地球上生活之前，鲸鱼就已经生活在海里了。

paragraphs.english: Now, there are not many whales.
paragraphs.chinese: 现在，鲸鱼不多了。

paragraphs.english: If we change now, whales and humans will have a bright future.
paragraphs.chinese: 如果我们现在改变，鲸鱼和人类将会有一个光明的未来。

paragraphs.english: Zip: Heal the world!
paragraphs.chinese: Zip：治愈世界！

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Make it a better place!
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom：让它成为一个更好的地方！

dialogues.speaker: Bill
dialogues.english: It's an elephant. It's big.

dialogues.speaker: Bill
dialogues.english: Guess! Is this bigger or smaller than the elephant?
dialogues.chinese: 猜猜看！这个比大象大还是小？

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: I think it's bigger than the elephant.

dialogues.speaker: Bill
dialogues.english: No, it isn't. It's smaller. Look. It's a tiger.
dialogues.chinese: 不，不是的。它更小。看。这是一只老虎。

dialogues.speaker: Bill
dialogues.english: Look. The other card is a whale.

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: So the whale is the biggest, and the tiger is the smallest.
dialogues.chinese: 所以鲸鱼是最大的，老虎是最小的。

content_blocks.scene_description: 迈克制作了一份关于自己学校生活的PPT（幻灯片），展示了他一周的课程安排及各科的活动内容。

key_points.english: Talk about school life, subjects, and favorite classes.
key_points.chinese: 谈论学校生活、科目和最喜欢的课程。

paragraphs.english: I go to school from Monday to Friday. My favourite day is Monday.
paragraphs.chinese: 我从星期一到星期五上学。我最喜欢的一天是星期一。

paragraphs.english: On Monday I have maths, English, music and PE. I like PE best.
paragraphs.chinese: 星期一我有数学、英语、音乐和体育课。我最喜欢体育课。

paragraphs.english: Maths is my first class. I often answer maths questions. Sometimes they're difficult.
paragraphs.chinese: 数学是我的第一节课。我经常回答数学问题。有时候它们很难。

paragraphs.english: In English class, I read many English stories, and I like to speak English with my friends.
paragraphs.chinese: 在英语课上，我读很多英语故事，而且我喜欢和朋友们说英语。

paragraphs.english: I play the piano or sing songs in music class. This is very noisy and often great fun.
paragraphs.chinese: 在音乐课上我弹钢琴或唱歌。这很吵闹但通常非常有趣。

paragraphs.english: In the afternoon I have PE class. I sometimes play football or exercise in the gym. I'm not good at running, but I like football best!
paragraphs.chinese: 下午我有体育课。我有时踢足球或在体育馆锻炼。我不擅长跑步，但我最喜欢足球！

key_points.english: Use 'be going to' and 'will' to talk about future events.
key_points.chinese: 使用'be going to'和'will'谈论未来的计划和事件。

paragraphs.english: Dear Friends,
paragraphs.chinese: 亲爱的朋友们，

paragraphs.english: It is time to leave our school. We are all going to middle school soon, so we will say goodbye to each other. We are going to have a farewell party. You can bring your friends.
paragraphs.chinese: 是时候离开我们的学校了。我们很快都要上中学了，所以我们将向彼此道别。我们要举办一个告别派对。你可以带上你的朋友。

paragraphs.english: Place: Our music room    Time: Friday 3 p.m.
paragraphs.chinese: 地点：我们的音乐教室    时间：星期五下午3点

paragraphs.english: Activities:
paragraphs.chinese: 活动：

paragraphs.english: We will draw pictures and sing songs. We are going to give prizes for the best pictures. We are going to have cake, candy and juice, too. Yum!
paragraphs.chinese: 我们将画画和唱歌。我们将为最好的画作颁发奖品。我们还会有蛋糕、糖果和果汁。真好吃！

paragraphs.english: We will also give many prizes to our good students. For example, who helped others the most? Who studied hard? Who wrote the best story?
paragraphs.chinese: 我们也将颁发许多奖项给我们的好学生。例如，谁帮助别人最多？谁学习努力？谁写了最好的故事？

paragraphs.english: We will listen to music, play games and dance. It will be great fun.
paragraphs.chinese: 我们将听音乐、玩游戏和跳舞。那将会很有趣。

paragraphs.english: See you at the party!
paragraphs.chinese: 派对上见！

content_blocks.scene_description: 一首歌谣，表达了在告别派对上，大家不唱悲伤的歌，而是要尽情唱歌、跳舞、玩耍，度过快乐的一天。

key_points.english: Express positive feelings and wishes for a farewell party.
key_points.chinese: 表达告别派对上的积极情感和祝愿。

paragraphs.english: The party is on Friday.
paragraphs.chinese: 派对在星期五。

paragraphs.english: It's time to say goodbye.
paragraphs.chinese: 是时候说再见了。

paragraphs.english: We won't sing any sad songs,
paragraphs.chinese: 我们不会唱任何悲伤的歌，

paragraphs.english: And we'll have lots of fun.
paragraphs.chinese: 我们将会有很多乐趣。

paragraphs.english: The party is on Friday.
paragraphs.chinese: 派对在星期五。

paragraphs.english: We'll dance and sing and play.
paragraphs.chinese: 我们将跳舞、唱歌和玩耍。

paragraphs.english: Don't be late for the party.
paragraphs.chinese: 参加派对不要迟到。

paragraphs.english: We'll have a happy, happy day.
paragraphs.chinese: 我们将度过快乐的一天。

content_blocks.scene_description: Zip 和 Zoom 在池塘边和草地上依依惜别。Zip 告诉 Zoom 自己要跟父亲去美国，并将上七年级，两人互相承诺保持联系并互道保重。

key_points.english: Understand the story and expressions used for parting and sharing news.
key_points.chinese: 理解故事内容以及用于离别和分享消息的表达。

paragraphs.english: Zip: I have some good news and some bad news.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 我有一些好消息和一些坏消息。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: What is it?
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 是什么事？

paragraphs.english: Zip: I'm going away for a long time.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 我要离开很长一段时间。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: I'm sorry to hear that.
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 听到这个我很难过。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Where are you going?
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 你要去哪里？

paragraphs.english: Zip: America. My father has a new job there.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 美国。我爸爸在那边有一份新工作。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: That is good news for your father. It's sad news for me.
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 这对你爸爸来说是个好消息。对我来说是个坏消息。

paragraphs.english: Zip: Me too.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 我也是。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Are you going to a new school?
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 你要去新学校吗？

paragraphs.english: Zip: I'm going to a new middle school. I'll be in Grade 7.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 我要去一所新的中学。我将上七年级。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Will you promise to write to me?
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 你能答应给我写信吗？

paragraphs.english: Zip: Of course.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 当然。

paragraphs.english: Zoom: Take care.
paragraphs.chinese: Zoom: 保重。

paragraphs.english: Zip: Take care.
paragraphs.chinese: Zip: 保重。

content_blocks.section_title: Unit 3 Tell me about your holiday
content_blocks.scene_description: A song about holiday activities.

paragraphs.english: Where did you go on your holiday, holiday, holiday?
paragraphs.chinese: 你假期去哪里了，假期，假期？

paragraphs.english: Did you swim? Did you shop?
paragraphs.chinese: 你游泳了吗？你购物了吗？

paragraphs.english: What did you do?
paragraphs.chinese: 你做了什么？

paragraphs.english: I went swimming in the sea, in the sea, in the sea.
paragraphs.chinese: 我去海里游泳了，在海里，在海里。

paragraphs.english: I went shopping near the sea.
paragraphs.chinese: 我在海边购物了。

paragraphs.english: I bought a gift for you.
paragraphs.chinese: 我给你买了一份礼物。

paragraphs.english: Where did you go on your holiday, holiday, holiday?
paragraphs.chinese: 你假期去哪里了，假期，假期？

paragraphs.english: Did you run? Did you jump?
paragraphs.chinese: 你跑步了吗？你跳跃了吗？

paragraphs.english: What did you do?
paragraphs.chinese: 你做了什么？

paragraphs.english: I rode a bike in the park, in the park, in the park.
paragraphs.chinese: 我在公园里骑自行车，在公园里，在公园里。

paragraphs.english: I didn't see a big round stone,
paragraphs.chinese: 我没有看到一块又大又圆的石头，

paragraphs.english: And ouch, I hurt my foot!
paragraphs.chinese: 哎哟，我伤到了我的脚！

content_blocks.scene_description: A song about personal changes over time.

paragraphs.english: Now I am taller, taller than before.
paragraphs.chinese: 现在我更高了，比以前更高。

paragraphs.english: Every day I grow bigger. Every day I grow more.
paragraphs.chinese: 每天我都长得更大。每天我都长得更多。

paragraphs.english: Once I was a young boy, younger than I am today.
paragraphs.chinese: 曾经我是一个小男孩，比今天更年轻。

paragraphs.english: Now I am a big boy, and I'm bigger every day.
paragraphs.chinese: 现在我是一个大男孩，而且我每天都在变大。

paragraphs.english: For school I get up early, earlier than before.
paragraphs.chinese: 为了上学我起得很早，比以前更早。

paragraphs.english: Every day I get up early, sometimes at four!
paragraphs.chinese: 每天我都起得很早，有时在四点！

paragraphs.english: Once I didn't talk a lot. I wasn't an active boy,
paragraphs.chinese: 曾经我不怎么说话。我不是一个活跃的男孩，

paragraphs.english: But now I talk more than before. I always talk with joy!
paragraphs.chinese: 但现在我比以前说得更多。我总是快乐地交谈！

content_blocks.section_title: Short plays - The three little pigs
content_blocks.scene_description: A short play about the three little pigs building their houses.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: Once upon a time, there were three little pigs, named Pete, Pat and Paul. They were brothers. One day, they left their mother's house. The winter was coming. They wanted to build a new house. One day, Pete met a man. He had straw to sell.
dialogues.chinese: 从前，有三只小猪，名叫皮特、帕特和保罗。他们是兄弟。有一天，他们离开了妈妈的房子。冬天就要来了。他们想建一座新房子。有一天，皮特遇到了一个男人。他有稻草要卖。

dialogues.speaker: Pete
dialogues.english: Can I please buy your straw?
dialogues.chinese: 请问我可以买你的稻草吗？

dialogues.speaker: Straw-seller
dialogues.english: Sure. What for?

dialogues.speaker: Pete
dialogues.english: I am going to build a house with it.
dialogues.chinese: 我打算用它来建一座房子。

dialogues.speaker: Straw-seller
dialogues.english: Straw is not strong.

dialogues.speaker: Pete
dialogues.english: That's OK. I need a house before winter.
dialogues.chinese: 没关系。我需要在冬天前有一座房子。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: So Pete built his house of straw. The next day, Pat met a man. He sold sticks.
dialogues.chinese: 所以皮特用稻草建了他的房子。第二天，帕特遇到了一个男人。他卖树枝。

dialogues.speaker: Pat
dialogues.english: Can I please buy your sticks?
dialogues.chinese: 请问我可以买你的树枝吗？

dialogues.speaker: Stick-seller
dialogues.english: Sure. What for?

dialogues.speaker: Pat
dialogues.english: I am going to build a house with them.
dialogues.chinese: 我打算用它们来建一座房子。

dialogues.speaker: Stick-seller
dialogues.english: Sticks are not strong.

dialogues.speaker: Pat
dialogues.english: That's OK. I need a house before winter.
dialogues.chinese: 没关系。我需要在冬天前有一座房子。

image_captions: Pete the pig talking to a man selling straw.
Pat the pig talking to a man selling sticks.

metadata.book: English (Primary School, Grade 6 · Volume 2), People's Education Press (PEP)

content_blocks.scene_description: The story of the three little pigs continues. Paul buys bricks to build his house. Later, a hungry wolf comes to Pete's straw house.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: So Pat built his house of sticks. Then Paul met a man. He sold bricks.
dialogues.chinese: 所以帕特用树枝建了他的房子。然后保罗遇到了一个男人。他卖砖。

dialogues.speaker: Paul
dialogues.english: Can I please buy your bricks?
dialogues.chinese: 请问我可以买你的砖吗？

dialogues.speaker: Brick-seller
dialogues.english: Sure. What for?

dialogues.speaker: Paul
dialogues.english: I am going to build a house with them.
dialogues.chinese: 我打算用它们建一座房子。

dialogues.speaker: Brick-seller
dialogues.english: Great! Bricks are very strong.

dialogues.speaker: Pete
dialogues.english: It is hard work to build a house of bricks.
dialogues.chinese: 用砖建房子是一项艰苦的工作。

dialogues.speaker: Pat
dialogues.english: Bricks are very heavy.

dialogues.speaker: Paul
dialogues.english:
Bricks are heavier than straw and sticks.
Brick houses are stronger than straw houses and stick houses.
dialogues.chinese: 砖比稻草和树枝重。砖房比茅草房和树枝房更坚固。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english:
The man sold Paul the bricks. Paul built his house of bricks.
Pat built his house of sticks. Pete built his house of straw.
Then one day, a hungry wolf came out of the woods. He went
to the first house. It was Pete's house, made of straw.
dialogues.chinese: 那个人把砖卖给了保罗。保罗用砖建了他的房子。帕特用树枝建了他的房子。皮特用稻草建了他的房子。然后有一天，一只饥饿的狼从树林里出来。他去了第一座房子。那是皮特的房子，用稻草做的。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Little Pig, Little Pig, let me in!

dialogues.speaker: Pete
dialogues.english: No way! No way! Not by the hair on my chin.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Then I will blow your house down.
dialogues.chinese: 那我就把你的房子吹倒。

image_captions: Paul the pig is talking to a brick-seller who has a cart full of bricks.
A wolf is blowing hard at a house made of straw, and the straw is flying away.

content_blocks.scene_description: The story of the Three Little Pigs.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf blew the straw house down. Pete ran to Pat's house. The wolf went to the second house. It was the house made of sticks.
dialogues.chinese: 狼把稻草房吹倒了。皮特跑到了帕特的房子里。狼去了第二座房子。那是用树枝做的房子。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Little Pig, Little Pig, let me in!

dialogues.speaker: Pete & Pat
dialogues.english: No way! No way! Not by the hair on my chin.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Then I will blow your house down.
dialogues.chinese: 那我就把你们的房子吹倒。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf blew the stick house down. Pete and Pat ran to Paul's house. So the wolf went to the brick house.
dialogues.chinese: 狼把树枝房吹倒了。皮特和帕特跑到了保罗的房子里。于是狼去了砖头房。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Little Pig, Little Pig, let me in!

dialogues.speaker: Pete, Pat & Paul
dialogues.english: No way! No way! Not by the hair on my chin.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Then I will blow your house down.
dialogues.chinese: 那我就把你们的房子吹倒。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf blew and blew. The house would not come down. He blew harder. The house would not come down. Finally, the wolf climbed on the top of the house. He went down the chimney. He fell into fire.
dialogues.chinese: 狼吹啊吹。房子就是不倒。他更用力地吹。房子还是不倒。最后，狼爬上了房顶。他从烟囱下去了。他掉进了火里。

dialogues.speaker: Pete, Pat & Paul
dialogues.english: Hurray!

The wolf trying to blow down the brick house.

content_blocks.scene_description: The beginning of the story of Little Red-Cap. Her mother asks her to take some cake and medicine to her grandmother.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: Once upon a time, there was a pretty little girl. She wore a little red cap. It was from her grandmother. The girl loved the cap very much. She was always wearing it. So everyone called her "Little Red-Cap". One day, her mother called her from the garden.
dialogues.chinese: 从前，有一个漂亮的小女孩。她戴着一顶小红帽。那是她奶奶给她的。女孩非常喜欢这顶帽子。她总是戴着它。所以大家都叫她“小红帽”。有一天，她妈妈从花园里叫她。

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.english: Little Red-Cap!

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Yes, Mum?

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.english: Here is a piece of sweet cake and some medicine. Please take them to your grandmother.
dialogues.chinese: 这里有一块甜蛋糕和一些药。请把它们带给你奶奶。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Sure!

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.english: Don't run off the path. Don't go into the woods. It's dangerous. Walk nicely and don't break the bottle. Don't forget to say "Good morning!" to your grandmother.
dialogues.chinese: 不要跑离小路。不要走进树林。那很危险。好好走，不要打破瓶子。别忘了对你奶奶说“早上好！”

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Yes, Mum. I will be careful.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: Little Red-Cap set out. She was so excited. She saw the woods. She wanted to go into the woods. She forgot her mother's words.
dialogues.chinese: 小红帽出发了。她太兴奋了。她看到了树林。她想走进树林。她忘记了妈妈的话。

image_captions: Little Red-Cap holding a basket and her mother standing on the steps near a white fence.

metadata.book: English (Primary School, Grade 6 · Volume 2), People's Education Press (PEP)

content_blocks.scene_description: The story of Little Red-Cap meeting the wolf in the woods.

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What's in the woods? I'd like to see the trees and the birds.
dialogues.chinese: 树林里有什么？我想看看树和鸟。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: She went into the woods. She saw a wolf.
dialogues.chinese: 她走进了树林。她看到了一只狼。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Good morning, Little Red-Cap.

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Good morning, Wolf.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Where are you going?

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: I'm going to my grandmother's house.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: What do you have there?

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: I have some cake and medicine for my grandmother.
dialogues.chinese: 我有一些蛋糕和药给我奶奶。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Where does your grandmother live?

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Her house is far. She lives near the mountain.
dialogues.chinese: 她的房子很远。她住在山附近。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Listen, Little Red-Cap. Can you hear the sweet little birds? They are singing. And look! How pretty the flowers are! Why don't you take some flowers to your grandmother?
dialogues.chinese: 听，小红帽。你能听到甜美的小鸟叫声吗？它们在唱歌。看！花儿多漂亮啊！你为什么不带些花给你奶奶呢？

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Good idea!

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: Little Red-Cap picked some flowers for her grandmother. The wolf ran to the grandmother's house. He knocked at the door.
dialogues.chinese: 小红帽为她奶奶摘了一些花。狼跑到了奶奶家。他敲了敲门。

dialogues.speaker: Grandmother
dialogues.english: Who is there?

image_captions: A white wolf sitting and talking to Little Red-Cap, who is holding a basket.

metadata.book: English (Primary School, Grade 6 · Volume 2), People's Education Press (PEP)

content_blocks.scene_description: The story of Little Red-Cap and the Wolf.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Little Red-Cap. It's me, Grandmother. I have some cake for you. Open the door, please.
dialogues.chinese: 小红帽。是我，外婆。我给你带了些蛋糕。请开门。

dialogues.speaker: Grandmother
dialogues.english: I'm in bed. Come in.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf went into the house.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Good morning, Grandmother.

dialogues.speaker: Grandmother
dialogues.english: What a strange voice you have, Little Red-Cap.
dialogues.chinese: 你的声音真奇怪，小红帽。

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: I have a cold. My throat is sore.

dialogues.speaker: Grandmother
dialogues.english: I'm sorry.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf jumped onto the bed and ate the grandmother. He got into the grandmother's bed. Little Red-Cap came to the house. She went into the house.
dialogues.chinese: 狼跳上床吃掉了外婆。他上了外婆的床。小红帽来到了房子。她走进了房子。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Good morning, Grandmother.

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: Good morning, my dear.

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What a strange voice you have, Grandmother!

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: I have a cold. My throat is sore.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: Little Red-Cap went to the bed. She saw the wolf. She was surprised.
dialogues.chinese: 小红帽走到床边。她看到了狼。她很惊讶。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What big eyes you have, Grandmother!

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: They help me see you better.

image_captions: The wolf is jumping onto the grandmother's bed.
Little Red-Cap is looking at the wolf in the grandmother's bed.

metadata.book: English (Primary School, Grade 6 · Volume 2), People's Education Press (PEP)

content_blocks.scene_description: The story of Little Red-Cap, the Wolf, the Hunter, and the Grandmother.

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What big ears you have, Grandmother!

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: They help me hear you better.
dialogues.chinese: 它们能让我听你听得更清楚。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What large hands you have, Grandmother!

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: They help me hug you better.
dialogues.chinese: 它们能让我更好地拥抱你。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: What big teeth you have, Grandmother!

dialogues.speaker: Wolf
dialogues.english: They help me eat you better.
dialogues.chinese: 它们能让我更好地吃掉你。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: The wolf jumped out of the bed. He ate Little Red-Cap. Then he went to sleep. A hunter came to the grandmother's house.
dialogues.chinese: 狼从床上跳了下来。他吃掉了小红帽。然后他去睡觉了。一个猎人来到了奶奶家。

dialogues.speaker: Hunter
dialogues.english: How does the old woman feel now? Is she better? I must go and see her.
dialogues.chinese: 老妇人现在感觉怎么样？她好些了吗？我必须去看看她。

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.english: He went to the grandmother's bed, but he didn't see her. He saw the wolf. He killed the wolf. Grandmother and Little Red-Cap jumped out of the wolf's stomach.
dialogues.chinese: 他走到奶奶的床前，但他没有看到她。他看到了狼。他杀死了狼。奶奶和小红帽从狼的肚子里跳了出来。

dialogues.speaker: Little Red-Cap
dialogues.english: Thank you, Mr Hunter. I will never go into the woods again by myself!
dialogues.chinese: 谢谢你，猎人先生。我再也不会自己一个人进树林了！

dialogues.speaker: Grandmother
dialogues.english: Thank you for helping us!

image_captions: Grandmother, Little Red-Cap, and the Hunter standing together, with the dead wolf lying on the ground nearby.

metadata.book: English (Primary School, Grade 6 · Volume 2), People's Education Press (PEP)
